metadata.title:
IMMENSE
SEWERAGE


SYSTEM

Planned for Fourth and
Fifth Wards at Base of


the North Hill.

PLANS BEING DRAWN

Trunk Sewer Proposed for
Area Covering Two


Square Miles.

pages.raw_text:
A gigantic sewer district, the
largest in the city, is contemplated
in resolution ADOPTED by the city
council and now IN the hands of
the city engineer 1t consists of
the building of a trunk sewer
through the Fourth and Fifth
wards at the base of the north hill.
The plan is to make the trunk sew
er large enough so that it will fur
nish sanitary drainage for a dis
trict two miles square and storm
water drainage for the low ground
at the base of the Lidgerwood and
North Monroe hill.


1t is proposed to have the sewer
begin near Division street and run
west through Corbin Park and pass
through the trestle under the
Northwest boulevard line of the
Washington Water Power COM
pany, on to the Spokane river. This
is a natural water course and the
sewer can be built there without
any unnecessary expense as to rock
work or deep cuts. The area ar
fected is so large that it is calcu-
lated that the individual cost per
lot will not be great.


When the figures as to cost are
obtained from the engineers office
the councilmen from the Fourth
and Fifth wards will see how the
proposition strikes the property
owners interested.


There is no sewer system in the
Corbin Park district or the terri
tory intervening between Carlisle
avenue aud the north hill at the
present time. The proposed sewer
will afford an outlet for this re-
gon, also for mile or more north
on the top of the hill.
